title: A different kind of party world
description: Recently I was scammed out of 400€, partly because of my own negligence, and partly because prepaidmobilerecharge.com is a fucking scam. About a year ago I got a grip on my finances and made a spreadsheet with all my monthly payments, and it seemed fine. About a month later I topped up 15€ on a prepaid mobile sim card. I did it in a hurry because I needed it very quick, and the normal Telekom service was taking ages. The money never even arrived in the end but whatever, eventually the normal service kicked in and I forgot about it.

Turns out, this third-party website I used secretly signed me up for a 50€ monthly subscription. I didn't check my bank's outgoing payments for many months and before I knew it, I lost a bunch of money. I managed to get some back, but I wasn't seeing that 400€ again unless I went to court or something. So dear readers, my advice to you is to turn on notifications from your bank app so you have a good overview of what money goes in and out of your account.

Anyway, around this time, my friend Ort asked me if I wanted to DJ at a disco party that his new company is putting on, and I decided to give it a go because a) it would be a fun experience being a professional DJ, b) always good to try new things and c) his company would pay me 400€ for it. Bam! I can solve this problem with the power of disco music. Perfect.

The party happened yesterday and it really was a fascinating look a different world. It went from 6pm until 9pm and was marketed as 'Gimme Gimme Disco - an ABBA-inspired Disco party'. It was marketed at like 50+ year olds and that was indeed the crowd. They were all there on the dot, mostly older women, either in groups of girlfriends or dragging their husbands along, and to be fair they all had great sparkly outfits. They also had the best time ever. I had a very easy time on the DJ decks pleasing them because I just had to play pop songs and they would go crazy. There was even a scripted moment where there were balloons when Dancing Queen came on half way through.

The whole thing was giving Kleinstadt-Deutschland. I wouldn't be surprised if they all went home to Brandenburg after the event. These are the kinds of events you see advertised on posters around town, and they sell out! It was 20€ a pop for the ticket so fair enough to Ort's company for making good buck from it. I think past me would have judged someone for going to a 6pm ABBA party but now I realise that they were happy and who am I to judge them. I was the DJ and I'm not about to judge myself for it.

Anyway that is this week's story time, I hope you enjoyed it. On the website I redesigned the 'Misc' page to be even less usable. Maybe the stuff on there is not meant to be actually clicked on, yknow. I've also been watching lots of films. As I write this I am still emotionally recovering from the most devastating film ever, Sirat. Honestly, I can't recommend it because it's that nightmarish. Go check your finances instead. See you next time.

entries.title: Promise Mascot Agency
description: I was a huge fan of the vibe-heavy Paradise Killer, and the devs of that made a new game so I gave it a go, but the music isn't as good. I didn't realise before buying that they have a new composer and yeah it's just not the same. Also, the vibe of the game is quite düster and grey/brown visually, and I don't love it.

The premise of the game is fucking wild. Whoever came up with this is insane. It's about a yakuza who owes millions and is banished to a cursed town to start a 'Mascot Agency'. In Japan there is a big culture of mascots, people wearing silly outfits to promote events, and in this game mascots are real, horrible monsters. You have to run the business with a massive finger called Pinky☆.

I don't think I'll complete this game. In the end it's a management sim and when the mascots need help on their events you have to do this little minigame which always has the same music and quickly gets boring. It constantly interrupts the normal gameplay, which is driving around in an indestructible truck. It's actually not a chill game at all.
entries.metadata: Vibes: 8, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

entries.title: Slay the Spire 2
description: Oh god here we go again. The first Slay the Spire kept me going for years, and I finally managed to spend the last year in peace having beaten A20 heart with all the characters. Now we've been served a whole-ass sequel with twice as much content. It's so over! I'll see you guys on the other side!!

I'm so excited to play this game in Early Access! The first time I've done that. I'm going to be there for each and every patch and it's going to be delicious. I've already identified the most broken cards and I look forward to the nerfs!
Also, I love the multiplayer! It works so good. So nice to bond with friends like this.
entries.metadata: Vibes: 7, Gameplay: 11, Platform: PC, Release: 2026

entries.title: Sirat
description: I will never forget this film. Watching it feels like a nightmare.
entries.metadata: Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: A World Not Ours
description: A film about Ein el-Helweh, a refugee camp in Lebanon filled with Palestinians. Fascinating. For me it was a reminder how fucked it is that refugees can't work. In this camp they are trapped there and they don't do any proper work because they don't have the papers. All over the world it's the same story, asylum seekers twiddle their thumbs in places like the UK when they could be working, and they want to work. It's fucked.
entries.metadata: Release: 2012

entries.title: Mar Mama
description: Upsetting short film about settler violence in the West Bank. Things like this are happening right now in Palestine. These horrible settlers are horrible and fuck the Israeli security in the region who support the settlers.
entries.metadata: Release: 2023

entries.title: Vibrations from Gaza
description: Upsetting short film about deaf kids in Gaza pre-October 7. There's a school of deaf kids there and I was just asking myself, has it been bombed now?
entries.metadata: Release: 2024

entries.title: The History of Sound
description: I was setting myself to not enjoy this because *rolls eyes* the power of music. But actually I got totally swept away by it. Loved it. Beautiful.
entries.metadata: Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: Pulp Fiction
description: A super masc, violent set of random stories with no real moral to the story. I watched it at home on my own for my education but didn't enjoy it.
entries.metadata: Release: 1994, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: The Mario Galaxy Movie
description: As a Nintendo fan I was contractually obliged to watch this, but it really is mainstream nonsense. It's a series of happenings and references with no real meaning. I did get every reference though, which was satisfying.
entries.metadata: Release: 2026, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: Pillion
description: Wow, this is a steaming hot film about getting dommed by sexy biker man Alexander Skarsgård. It handles the topic excellently, is super funny, and I highly recommend it. Managed to get a free T-shirt from the Mongay preview!
entries.metadata: Release: 2026, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: No Other Choice
description: Had a solo trip to the cinema (Delphi Lux) while I was already feeling sad and upset and this just made it worse. It's an excellent film but wow, the plot really is upsetting for so many reasons.
entries.metadata: Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: If My Wife New I'd Be Dead
description: Having seen CMAT live and been absolutely blown away, I have been absolutely peaking on my CMAT obsession. This is her first album and it's full of the most real music. It's very country and western which is not normally something I'm into but hell yeah. It's all about 'I Wanna Be a Cowboy, Baby!' which sounds like it's from that vine (which it is), but it's actually a song about frustration with being a woman (among other things). It's great and I love it. She's also unafraid to get dark - 'Nashville' references this person who spent 8 months telling everyone they're moving away and after saying their goodbyes they killed themselves. Knowing that completely transforms the listening experience of that song for sure.
entries.metadata: Artist: CMAT, Release: 2022, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

entries.title: Splatoon 3
description: I don't like Splatoon music, I just don't think it's good. The weird voices, the not-very-good pop music. I hadn't even given this soundtrack much of a chance, but decided to return to it when they updated in 2026 with the DLC music.

It's still shit. It's all over the place. If it doesn't have the weird vocals, it's the most extreme, out-there noise music. If I were to listen to this on drugs I would have a meltdown. Just as an experiment, listen to 'Deluge Dirge' which is from the Salmon Run mode.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Super Mario Bros. Wonder
description: This game is really fun to play, but the music is ass. I had high hopes because I actually love the New Super Mario Bros music, but there's nothing good here really. I was excited by the trailer in the music because of the funny voice, but they only used it for like one track, such a disappointment.
In 2026 they added the Switch 2 version with Bellabel Park and honestly there's not much there. For a game so visually vibey, the music just doesn't reflect the vibes.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Super Metroid
description: This soundtrack is full of classics. It truly is the very best Metroid soundtrack and one of the best on the SNES. Personal favourites are the two Brinstar tracks. The first one is a groove and a bop, and the second is the ultimate unsettling chillout. Also shout-outs to the most insane boss music ever with Mother Brain. Wow.
entries.metadata: Platform: SNES, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: CMAT
description: This genuinely might have been one of the best concerts of my life. I went to see CMAT right at the height of my CMAT obsession, so it was a total pleasure to just bathe in her glory surrounded by all these well-dressed gays in the crowd! She is so funny, has such stage banter, would just stop songs to pose for a bit, she even spontaneously changed the setlist to accommodate requests from children in the audience. She did an interpretative dance of her experience not getting into Berghain! Also, a woman asked me out on a date at this concert. Highlight of the year for me.
entries.metadata: Date: 26-03-2026, Venue: Tempodrom, Berlin
